metadata.summary: On the night of October 14, 2023, 22-year-old Anjali Verma, a final-year law student, reported being sexually assaulted in the parking lot of her apartment complex in Sector 17, Chandigarh. She claimed she was approached by a man who pretended to be a security guard, forced her into a secluded corner, and raped her under threat of violence. She managed to escape and reported the incident to the Sector 17 Police Station within 90 minutes. The victim’s phone records show three calls to her roommate, Priya Mehta, and one to a number registered under the name of Rohan Kapoor, a local gym trainer and neighbor. CCTV footage from the main gate shows Anjali entering at 10:12 PM and exiting at 10:48 PM, but the camera covering the parking lot was malfunctioning between 10:20 PM and 10:50 PM. A medical examination confirmed sexual assault and collected biological evidence, including a partial DNA profile from under Anjali’s fingernails. The forensic report matched the DNA to Rohan Kapoor, but he claims he was at a late-night yoga session with his instructor, Arjun Singh, and provides a timestamped receipt from a nearby café. Anjali’s family, influential in local politics, pressured the police to expedite the case, while Rohan’s family alleges the accusation is a cover-up for a failed romantic relationship. The case is further complicated by conflicting witness statements: one neighbor claims to have seen Anjali walking with a man she knew, while another insists she was alone. The victim’s initial statement to police described the assailant as wearing a black hoodie, but later told her counselor the man was wearing a blue jacket — a detail never mentioned to police.

hidden_ground_truth.true_motive: Revenge for Anjali rejecting his romantic advances and threatening to expose his history of harassing female gym members
true_timeline: 2023-10-14 10:12 PM: Anjali Verma enters apartment complex parking lot.
2023-10-14 10:18 PM: Rohan Kapoor, disguised in a black hoodie, approaches Anjali under pretense of checking security.
2023-10-14 10:21 PM: Rohan drags Anjali behind a storage shed, assaults her, and forces her to bite him during struggle.
2023-10-14 10:35 PM: Rohan flees, discards hoodie in nearby trash bin, and walks to Café Zenith.
2023-10-14 10:37 PM: Rohan arrives at Café Zenith, where Arjun Singh (his yoga instructor) is waiting. Arjun falsifies the receipt timestamp to 10:30 PM to support alibi.
2023-10-14 10:48 PM: Anjali escapes and reports incident.
2023-10-14 11:05 PM: Rohan calls Arjun to confirm alibi alignment.
lying_witnesses: Mrs. Mehta (Anjali’s neighbor who claims to have seen her with a man she knew)
forged_evidence: Timestamp-forged receipt from Café Zenith (original receipt shows 10:37 PM, altered to 10:30 PM)
Deleted call log from Rohan’s phone to Anjali’s number at 9:45 PM — later recovered by forensic team — indicating prior contact

injected_contradictions.description: Victim initially told police assailant wore black hoodie; later told counselor blue jacket — but CCTV from building entrance shows victim wearing blue jacket herself, leading to confusion over description.
Rohan’s DNA found under victim’s nails — but victim’s roommate Priya claims Anjali had scratched her own arm earlier that day while gardening, suggesting possible contamination.
Call log shows Rohan called Anjali at 9:45 PM — Rohan claims it was a wrong number; victim says she ignored it, but forensic analysis shows the call lasted 47 seconds and was not blocked.

contradictions.claim_a: Rohan Kapoor claims he was at Café Zenith from 10:30 PM to 11:00 PM, supported by a timestamped receipt.
contradictions.claim_b: Café Zenith’s server logs show the receipt was printed at 10:37 PM, and the POS system was manually altered — the actual time Rohan arrived was 10:37 PM, making his alibi impossible.

contradictions.claim_a: Anjali Verma told police the attacker wore a black hoodie.
contradictions.claim_b: Anjali later told her counselor the attacker wore a blue jacket — a detail never disclosed to police, but her own jacket was blue, creating ambiguity about whether she misremembered or was misled.

contradictions.claim_a: DNA under Anjali’s fingernails matches Rohan Kapoor’s profile.
contradictions.claim_b: Anjali’s roommate Priya Mehta claims Anjali scratched her own arm while gardening at 6 PM, suggesting the DNA could be from self-inflicted injury, not assault.

contradictions.claim_a: Rohan Kapoor’s phone log shows no call to Anjali on October 14.
contradictions.claim_b: Forensic recovery from backup shows a 47-second call from Rohan to Anjali at 9:45 PM, which was manually deleted from the active log.

contradictions.claim_a: Mrs. Mehta, neighbor, claims she saw Anjali walking with a man she recognized as a neighbor around 10:20 PM.
contradictions.claim_b: Security guard on duty confirms Mrs. Mehta was not on her balcony at that time due to a migraine — her testimony is based on memory, not direct observation.

documents.content: I, Anjali Verma, daughter of Mr. Rajesh Verma, resident of Apartment 4B, Sector 17, Chandigarh, hereby lodge this complaint against an unknown male assailant. On the night of 14th October, 2023, at approximately 10:20 PM, while returning to my apartment after a late study session at the library, I was approached by a man near the parking lot who identified himself as a security personnel. He asked if I needed assistance, and when I replied affirmatively, he suddenly grabbed me by the arm and dragged me behind a storage shed. He threatened to kill me if I screamed or resisted. He then forcibly had sexual intercourse with me. During the struggle, I bit his hand and scratched his face and arms. I managed to escape at around 10:45 PM and ran to the main gate, where I reported the incident to the night guard. I have no prior acquaintance with the assailant. He was wearing a black hoodie, dark jeans, and a mask covering his lower face. I could not see his face clearly due to the darkness and my fear. I request immediate investigation and protection. I have undergone medical examination at Apollo Hospital, Chandigarh, and the report is attached. I am filing this FIR within 90 minutes of the incident as per legal requirement.

I was returning home from the university library around 10:15 PM. I entered the parking lot of my apartment complex and was walking toward my building when a man in a black hoodie stepped out from behind a parked car. He said, 'Ma'am, I'm the night security guard. Is everything okay?' I said yes, but he grabbed me and pulled me behind the storage shed. He had a low voice, smelled of sweat and gym equipment. He forced me to have sex. I fought back — I scratched him and bit his left hand. He told me if I screamed, he’d kill me and my family. I escaped when he turned to tie his shoe. I ran to the main gate and called my roommate Priya immediately. I told the police he wore a black hoodie. I didn’t mention the blue jacket because I thought it was irrelevant. I didn’t know the man. I’ve never met him before. I did receive a call from an unknown number at 9:45 PM that night, but I ignored it. I didn’t recognize the number.

metadata.occupation: Certified Gym Trainer, FitLife Gym, Sector 17

documents.content: I was at Café Zenith from 10:30 PM to 11:00 PM on October 14, 2023, with my yoga instructor, Arjun Singh. We were discussing meditation techniques after our late-night session. I have the receipt from the café as proof. I did not meet Anjali Verma that night. I don’t even know her well — we’ve exchanged greetings in the elevator. I called her once by mistake at 9:45 PM — I thought I was calling my cousin. I didn’t realize I’d dialed her number until later. I have no motive to harm her. I’ve never harassed anyone. I’m being framed because my family is poor and hers is politically powerful. I don’t know how my DNA got under her nails. Maybe she scratched herself gardening earlier that day — I heard from neighbors she was doing that in the afternoon.

I conducted a late-night yoga session from 9:30 PM to 10:30 PM at Zenith Wellness Center. Afterward, I went to Café Zenith for a herbal tea. Rohan Kapoor joined me at 10:30 PM. We sat together until 11:00 PM. I personally witnessed him ordering tea at 10:30 PM. The receipt was printed at that time — I saw the printer activate. I can confirm his alibi. I have no knowledge of any incident involving Anjali Verma. I’ve never met her. I don’t know why the police are questioning me. I only know Rohan as a dedicated student of yoga.

I was at home when Anjali called me at 10:48 PM, crying. She said she’d been raped. I called the police immediately. I’ve known Anjali for three years. She’s a straight-A student, never reckless. Earlier that day, around 6 PM, she was gardening in the complex garden and scratched her left forearm badly while trimming thorny bushes. She showed me the scratch — it was bleeding. I told her to clean it. She didn’t mention the assault until later. I don’t know Rohan Kapoor personally, but I’ve seen him around. I don’t know why his DNA is under her nails — maybe it’s from the gardening scratch? She didn’t tell me about the blue jacket. I think she was confused after the trauma.

I saw Anjali walking with a man around 10:20 PM. I was on my balcony, sipping tea. I recognized the man — he’s a neighbor, works at the gym. He was wearing a blue jacket. They were talking calmly. I thought they were going to the café together. I didn’t think anything was wrong. I didn’t see any struggle. I didn’t call the police because I thought it was consensual. I didn’t know about the assault until the next morning. I didn’t see the man’s face clearly — just the jacket. I have a migraine, so I don’t remember exact details. But I’m sure it was a man she knew.

My daughter is a law student with a bright future. She was attacked in our own community, in broad view of security cameras — which, of course, were conveniently malfunctioning. We have no doubt this is a targeted crime. We have no grudge against any individual, but we demand justice. We have spoken to senior police officers to ensure this case is handled with urgency and sensitivity. We are not pressuring anyone — we are simply insisting on due process. We have full faith in the system. We have provided all medical and digital records. We expect no delays.

My son is a good boy. He works hard. He has no criminal record. He’s been practicing yoga for two years. He doesn’t even drink. Why is he being targeted? Because Anjali’s father is a government officer? Because we are middle-class and they are influential? They are twisting everything. My son called her once — a wrong number. He didn’t even know her name. The DNA? Maybe she touched something he touched. Maybe the gardener’s blood got mixed. I don’t know. But my son is innocent. The café receipt proves it. The police should investigate the neighbor who saw her with someone else. Why aren’t they questioning her?

metadata.analyst: Constable Vikram Singh, Cyber Cell, Sector 17 PS

documents.content: CCTV footage from Main Gate Camera (Camera ID: SG-17-MG-03) shows Anjali Verma entering the complex at 10:12:14 PM wearing a blue denim jacket, black leggings, and carrying a backpack. She walks directly toward her building (Apartment 4B). No other individuals are seen entering with her. The camera covering the parking lot (Camera ID: SG-17-PL-07) was offline from 10:20:03 PM to 10:50:17 PM due to a power surge. No footage exists of the alleged incident location. The camera system logs confirm the outage was not caused by tampering — it was a documented system failure reported by the housing society at 10:18 PM. No evidence of manual intervention found.

metadata.analyst: Dr. Nidhi Sharma, Senior Forensic Scientist

documents.content: Biological samples collected from under the fingernails of victim Anjali Verma (Sample ID: FV-1014-23) were subjected to STR analysis. The DNA profile obtained (Allele Profile: D3S1358-15,17; TH01-6,9; D21S11-28,30; etc.) matches the reference sample of Rohan Kapoor (Sample ID: RK-1015-23) with a match probability of 1 in 1.2 billion. No other contributors detected. The sample contained trace epithelial cells consistent with a struggle. No blood or other bodily fluids were present. The sample was collected 4 hours post-incident. Contamination from prior injury (gardening scratch) cannot be ruled out as a contributing factor, but the quantity and distribution of DNA are consistent with direct contact during violent struggle. Conclusion: DNA profile matches Rohan Kapoor. Source of DNA is likely from physical contact during assault.

metadata.physician: Dr. Meera Gupta, Forensic Medicine Department

documents.content: Patient: Anjali Verma, 22F. Presented at 11:30 PM on 14.10.2023. History: Sexual assault reported. Examination: External genitalia shows signs of trauma — abrasions on labia minora, perineal tear consistent with forced penetration. Hymen is intact but stretched. Multiple bruises on inner thighs and left upper arm. Fingernail scrapings collected. Bite mark on left hand — consistent with victim’s dentition. No semen detected. No foreign DNA on vaginal swabs. No signs of recent consensual intercourse. Patient reports being threatened with violence. Psychological assessment: Acute stress reaction. Medical opinion: Sexual assault confirmed. Injuries consistent with non-consensual act under duress. DNA evidence from fingernails is critical for identification.

metadata.lab: Digital Evidence Unit, Chandigarh Cyber Cell

documents.content: Forensic imaging of Anjali Verma’s iPhone 13 (Serial: F123456) recovered call logs showing three calls to Priya Mehta (10:48 PM, 10:50 PM, 10:52 PM) and one call to Rohan Kapoor (10:49 PM, duration: 12 seconds). Call log from Rohan Kapoor’s Samsung S21 (Serial: G789012) showed no outgoing call to Anjali on 14.10.2023 in active log. However, forensic recovery from device backup (cloud sync, 13.10.2023) revealed a deleted call log entry: Call to Anjali Verma (9:45:03 PM, duration: 47 seconds). Call was manually deleted using third-party app 'Call Eraser Pro'. No call records from Rohan to Arjun Singh on that date. WhatsApp and SMS logs show no prior communication between Rohan and Anjali. Anjali’s phone GPS data shows she was at library until 10:05 PM, then walked home. Rohan’s phone GPS shows movement from FitLife Gym to Sector 17 parking lot between 10:15–10:25 PM, then to Café Zenith at 10:37 PM.

Café Zenith POS system logs show a transaction for two herbal teas and one almond milk latte on 14.10.2023 at 10:37:12 PM. Receipt printed at 10:37:15 PM. The receipt presented as evidence (Receipt ID: CZ-1014-23-089) was manually altered — the timestamp was changed from 10:37:15 PM to 10:30:00 PM using administrative override. The override was performed using the manager’s credentials, which were accessed by Arjun Singh on 15.10.2023 at 3:15 AM. The system logs confirm the original receipt was printed at 10:37 PM. No other transactions from Rohan Kapoor were recorded before or after. CCTV from café entrance shows Rohan Kapoor entering at 10:37:08 PM, Arjun Singh already seated.

Investigation Summary: Victim Anjali Verma reported rape on 14.10.2023. CCTV at parking lot was offline during critical window. Forensic DNA matches accused Rohan Kapoor. Alibi provided via Café Zenith receipt, but POS logs prove receipt was forged. Deleted call log recovered — 47-second call from Rohan to victim at 9:45 PM, unexplained. Witness Mrs. Mehta’s testimony contradicted by security guard’s log — she was not on balcony. Victim’s roommate confirms victim had gardening injury earlier. Medical report confirms assault. Digital forensics places Rohan at scene between 10:15–10:25 PM. Arjun Singh admitted to altering receipt under pressure from Rohan. Victim’s statement about blue jacket inconsistent with police report — she later disclosed to counselor she was wearing blue jacket herself. This may have caused confusion. Conclusion: Sufficient evidence to charge Rohan Kapoor under Section 376 IPC. Arjun Singh is under investigation for tampering with evidence. Case is complex due to conflicting testimonies and influence from both families. Recommend prosecution.

IN THE COURT OF THE SESSIONS JUDGE, CHANDIGARH

CHARGE SHEET UNDER SECTION 376 IPC

Accused: Rohan Kapoor, S/o Ramesh Kapoor, aged 26, resident of Sector 17, Chandigarh.

1. On 14.10.2023 at approximately 10:20 PM, the accused, disguised in a black hoodie, approached the victim Anjali Verma in the parking lot of her apartment complex under the pretense of being a security guard.

2. He forcibly dragged her behind a storage shed, threatened her with death, and committed penetrative sexual assault.

3. During the struggle, the victim scratched the accused’s hands and face, resulting in transfer of epithelial DNA under her fingernails, which was forensically matched to the accused.

4. The accused attempted to fabricate an alibi by forging a café receipt from Café Zenith, altering the timestamp from 10:37 PM to 10:30 PM with the assistance of Arjun Singh.

5. The accused made a 47-second call to the victim at 9:45 PM, which was deleted from his phone log — indicating prior contact and possible stalking.

6. The accused had previously been reported by two female gym members for inappropriate touching and verbal harassment — evidence under Section 11 of the Indian Evidence Act.

The accused is charged under Section 376 IPC (Rape), Section 193 IPC (False Evidence), Section 201 IPC (Destruction of Evidence), and Section 506 IPC (Criminal Intimidation). Arjun Singh is separately charged under Section 193 and 201 IPC. The victim’s testimony and forensic evidence are consistent and corroborated by digital records. The alibi is proven false. The case is ready for trial.

queries.question: What evidence was forged, and by whom?
Why is the timeline of the café receipt critical to the case?
Which witness statements are intentionally deceptive, and what details were omitted or falsified?
How does the deleted call log at 9:45 PM impact the motive analysis?
What contradiction exists between the victim’s description of the assailant’s clothing and her own attire?
Is the DNA evidence under the victim’s fingernails conclusive, or could it have been contaminated?
What is the relationship between Arjun Singh and Rohan Kapoor, and how does it affect the case?
What is the likelihood of conviction based on the available evidence?
How did family pressure influence the investigation process?

expected_answers.answer: Rohan Kapoor is the actual perpetrator. The forensic DNA under the victim’s fingernails matches his profile. Digital forensics place him at the parking lot between 10:15–10:25 PM, and his phone was used to call the victim 47 seconds earlier at 9:45 PM — indicating prior contact and intent. His alibi was fabricated using a forged café receipt, which was timestamped by Arjun Singh to 10:30 PM, while the POS system logs prove he arrived at 10:37 PM. His motive is revenge for being rejected by Anjali and her threat to expose his history of harassing female gym members.

The Café Zenith receipt was forged by Arjun Singh, at the request of Rohan Kapoor. The original receipt was printed at 10:37:15 PM, but was manually altered to 10:30:00 PM using administrative access. This was done to create a false alibi. Additionally, Rohan Kapoor deleted the 47-second call log to Anjali from his phone using a third-party app, which was later recovered by digital forensics — this constitutes destruction of evidence.

The café receipt timeline is critical because it forms the core of Rohan Kapoor’s alibi. If he was at the café at 10:30 PM, he could not have committed the assault between 10:20–10:35 PM. However, the POS system logs prove the receipt was printed at 10:37 PM, meaning he arrived at the café after the assault. This makes his alibi impossible and proves premeditated fabrication of evidence, directly implicating him in the crime and obstructing justice.

Arjun Singh and Mrs. Mehta are intentionally deceptive. Arjun Singh falsely testified that Rohan arrived at Café Zenith at 10:30 PM and that the receipt was printed then — he later admitted to altering the timestamp. Mrs. Mehta claimed she saw Anjali walking with a man she recognized at 10:20 PM — but the security guard confirmed she was not on her balcony at that time due to migraine, meaning her testimony is fabricated or based on false memory. Both are protecting Rohan Kapoor.

The deleted 47-second call at 9:45 PM indicates prior contact between Rohan and Anjali, contradicting his claim of never knowing her. The duration suggests a deliberate conversation, not a wrong number. This supports the motive of revenge for rejection — he was likely attempting to rekindle a romantic connection or warn her against exposing his harassment. The deletion shows intent to conceal, strengthening the case for premeditation and stalking behavior.

The victim initially told police the assailant wore a black hoodie, but later told her counselor he wore a blue jacket. However, CCTV shows the victim herself was wearing a blue jacket. This creates ambiguity — was she misremembering the attacker’s clothing, or was she subconsciously projecting her own attire onto the assailant due to trauma? This contradiction was exploited by the defense to cast doubt, but it does not negate the DNA evidence or timeline.

The DNA evidence is forensically conclusive — the match probability is 1 in 1.2 billion. While Priya Mehta claims the victim scratched herself gardening earlier, the forensic report notes the quantity and distribution of DNA under the fingernails are consistent with a violent struggle, not a minor scratch. The sample contained epithelial cells from multiple areas of the hand, not localized to a single wound. Contamination is theoretically possible but statistically negligible given the profile strength and context.

Arjun Singh is Rohan Kapoor’s yoga instructor and close confidant. He provided emotional support to Rohan and was aware of his resentment toward Anjali. He assisted in forging the café receipt to create an alibi and may have advised Rohan on how to evade suspicion. Their relationship is one of mentorship and complicity — Arjun’s actions were not merely passive but actively obstructive to justice.

The likelihood of conviction is very high. The case has multiple converging lines of evidence: DNA match, digital forensics placing the accused at the scene, a proven fabricated alibi, a deleted incriminating call, and testimony from the victim corroborated by medical findings. While witness contradictions exist, they are largely attributable to deception by accomplices or trauma-induced memory errors. The prosecution’s case is strong and meets the standard of 'beyond reasonable doubt'.

Family pressure from Anjali’s father, a senior government official, expedited the investigation and ensured forensic reports were prioritized. This prevented delays but also created public perception of bias. Conversely, Rohan’s family pressured authorities to focus on the neighbor’s testimony and the gardening injury, attempting to divert suspicion. While pressure did not alter forensic results, it influenced the tone of media coverage and the speed of procedural steps, highlighting systemic vulnerability to influence in high-profile cases.
